# Voice Guide — The Cartographer's Silence

## Narrative Voice

Third-person limited. Precise, observational prose with an undercurrent of institutional dread. The narrator notices details the way a cartographer would — measurements, spatial relationships, the gap between what is recorded and what is real.

## Prose Style

- Short declarative sentences for moments of realization
- Longer, winding sentences for description and world-building
- Metaphors drawn from cartography, measurement, and institutional language
- Minimal adverbs; let verbs carry the weight
- Dialogue is sparse and functional — characters speak to convey information, not to fill silence

## POV Voices

### Dorren Hayle
Precise, controlled, emotionally restrained. She processes the world through systems and categories. When overwhelmed, her language becomes more clinical, not less.

### Tessa Merrin
Sensory, curious, slightly irreverent. She notices textures, smells, sounds. Her internal voice has an edge of dark humor.
